hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 8, 3. You decided to call after your opponent raised before the flop, hoping to get a better read on their hand.
Flop — Ace, 10, 4. Your opponent came out betting, and you chose to call, trying to stay in the hand and gather more information.
Turn — 5. The board didn't change much for you, and your opponent bet again with a bigger sizing, putting more pressure on your decision.
River — 3. The final card fell, and your opponent fired a large river bet, forcing you to make a tough decision with the hand you had.

wrongReasons: 7 6 makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only one pair, 3 K wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
With only one pair, 7 4 wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: A 4

Your opponent flopped two pair and maintained consistent pressure with their bets on all three streets, indicating a strong hand that needed protection but wasn't an overwhelming favorite. The fact that they didn't slow down on the turn or river suggests they had a hand like A 4 that made two pair on the flop and didn't improve or deteriorate thereafter.
